name: DFS Flood Fill (explicit stack)
summary: Flood each island with an iterative depth-first stack, count its cells, and keep the largest area seen.
visualizationIntent: init: draw the grid with water dimmed and land idle, empty state overlay, empty stack, best=0. loop: scan for unvisited land. check: test whether the scanned cell is unvisited land. update: on new land start area=0, flood it counting each cell (area increments), and after flooding update best=max(best,area). move: pop a cell (current), mark it done, push its unvisited land neighbors as frontier. return: show the largest area found.

lineExplanations.3: Record how many rows the grid has.
lineExplanations.4: Record how many columns each row has.
lineExplanations.5: Start an empty 'state' tracker (0 = unseen, 1 = in the stack, 2 = done).
lineExplanations.6: Loop over each row to give it a matching tracker row.
lineExplanations.7: Add a fresh row of zeros so every cell starts unseen.
lineExplanations.8: Start the best-area answer at 0 (no island seen yet).
lineExplanations.9: Placeholder for the current cell's row; -1 means 'none yet'.
lineExplanations.10: Placeholder for the current cell's column; -1 means 'none yet'.
lineExplanations.11: The four orthogonal moves — up, down, right, left.
lineExplanations.12: Scan every row looking for land to start a new island.
lineExplanations.13: Within the row, scan every column left to right.
lineExplanations.14: Is this a new island? True only when the cell is land (1) and unseen.
lineExplanations.15: Reset this island's running area to 0 before flooding it.
lineExplanations.16: Create an empty stack for this island's cells to explore.
lineExplanations.17: Push the island's starting cell onto the stack.
lineExplanations.18: Mark the starting cell as seen so it isn't pushed twice.
lineExplanations.19: Keep flooding while the stack has cells left to explore.
lineExplanations.20: Pop the most recently added cell (depth-first).
lineExplanations.23: Mark this cell fully done — it belongs to the current island.
lineExplanations.24: Count this cell: the current island's area grows by one.
lineExplanations.25: Try each of the four directions from this cell.
lineExplanations.28: Is the neighbor valid, un-reached land? (in-bounds, is 1, still unseen).
lineExplanations.29: Mark that land neighbor as seen before pushing it.
lineExplanations.30: Push the neighbor to explore later — the island keeps growing.
lineExplanations.31: Island fully measured — keep it if its area beats the best so far.
lineExplanations.32: Clear the current-row marker so the cursor hides while we scan on.
lineExplanations.34: All cells scanned — return the largest island area found.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: is the class wrapper LeetCode expects.
syntaxExplanations.2: def maxAreaOfIsland(self, grid): defines the method; grid is the 2D list of 0/1 integers.
syntaxExplanations.4: grid[0] is the first row; len(...) gives the column count.
syntaxExplanations.5: [] creates an empty list to fill with tracker rows.
syntaxExplanations.6: for r in range(rows): loops r over each row index.
syntaxExplanations.7: [0] * cols builds a row of that many zeros; .append adds it.
syntaxExplanations.8: best = 0 initializes the answer to zero.
syntaxExplanations.9: cur_r = -1 sets a sentinel; -1 is not a valid index.
syntaxExplanations.10: cur_c = -1 does the same for the column.
syntaxExplanations.11: A list of [row-delta, col-delta] pairs for the 4 directions.
syntaxExplanations.12: for sr in range(rows): loops the scan row index.
syntaxExplanations.13: for sc in range(cols): loops the scan column index.
syntaxExplanations.14: and requires both parts true; grid[sr][sc] indexes row then column.
syntaxExplanations.16: [] makes an empty list used as a stack.
syntaxExplanations.17: append([sr, sc]) pushes a [row, col] pair.
syntaxExplanations.19: while len(stack) > 0: runs while the stack isn't empty.
syntaxExplanations.20: stack.pop() removes and returns the top item.
syntaxExplanations.21: cell[0] reads the pair's first element (row).
syntaxExplanations.23: state[cur_r][cur_c] = 2 marks the cell fully processed.
syntaxExplanations.24: area = area + 1 increases the island's cell count.
syntaxExplanations.25: for d in dirs: loops over each direction.
syntaxExplanations.26: cur_r + d[0] gives the neighbor's row.
syntaxExplanations.27: cur_c + d[1] gives the neighbor's column.
syntaxExplanations.28: Chained 'and' checks bounds, that it's land (1), and unseen.
syntaxExplanations.30: append([nr, nc]) queues the neighbor to explore.
syntaxExplanations.31: max(best, area) keeps the larger of the two.
syntaxExplanations.34: return best hands back the final answer.
